title: CRISPR's CTX112 Cancer Therapy Shows 67% Response Rate in Clinical Trial, Earns FDA Special Status
text: CRISPR Therapeutics CTX112 Clinical Trial Summary. Key Clinical Data: 67% objective response rate across all dose levels. 50% complete response rate. Five patients achieved responses lasting over 6 months. One patient remained in complete remission over one year post-infusion. Safety Profile: The therapy demonstrated favorable tolerability with no Grade 3 or greater infections and no cases of graft versus host disease reported. All cytokine release syndrome and neurotoxicity events were Grade 1-2. Regulatory Milestone: FDA granted Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy (RMAT) designation for CTX112 in treating relapsed or refractory follicular lymphoma and marginal zone lymphoma. Trial Specifics: The Phase 1/2 study enrolled 12 subjects with heavily pre-treated B-cell malignancies across four dose levels (30-600 million CAR+ T cells). Patients were enriched for high-risk features, including 75% with primary refractory disease or early relapse. Future Outlook: CRISPR indicated CTX112 is also being investigated in systemic lupus erythematosus with potential to expand into additional autoimmune indications. A comprehensive update across oncology and autoimmune programs is expected mid-2025. Market Reaction: CRSP stock declined 4.76% on publication day.
